title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is building an autonomous agent pipeline that converts product signals like errors, rage clicks, and customer messages into pull requests. The system groups related signals using LLM-generated query embeddings, researches codebases, and opens PRs automatically.
writeup: Joshua Snyder of PostHog describes an autonomous agent pipeline that turns product signals (errors, rage clicks, and customer messages) into pull requests. The system groups related signals using LLM-generated query embeddings, researches the relevant codebases, and opens PRs automatically. The talk covers the supporting techniques: AI agents, embeddings, test-time compute, and error recovery. The takeaway is a concrete architecture for moving from raw product feedback to code changes without manual triage.
